title: HOW TO READ WIRING DIAGRAMS

figures_detected.description: Example diagram showing how the Super Multiple Junction (S.M.J.) is represented in simplified wiring diagrams and how to cross-reference to the terminal arrangement foldout. Shows STARTING SYSTEM wiring diagram, S.M.J. simplified representation with main harness and instrument harness connections, and the full terminal arrangement grid of the S.M.J.

content_blocks.text: The "S.M.J." indicated in wiring diagrams is shown in a simplified form. The terminal arrangement should therefore be referred to in the foldout at the end of the Service Manual.
The foldout should be spread to read the entire wiring diagram.

content_blocks.description: Example showing S.M.J. usage in wiring diagrams. The simplified wiring diagram shows S.M.J. connections between Main harness and Instrument harness. The S.M.J. label instructs: 'Refer to last page (Foldout page).' The terminal arrangement grid on the right shows the full S.M.J. connector layout with terminals labeled A0-A9, B0-B9, C0-C9, D0-D9, E0-E9, F0-F9, G0-G9 on the main harness side and G0-G9, F0-F9, E0-E9, D0-D9, C0-C9, B0-B9, A0-A9 on the instrument harness side.

callouts.label: S.M.J. Refer to last page (Foldout page).
